frame_analyses.description: Initial setup view showing Penrose drain with slit and marked target points positioned on red felt platform
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear view of the two blue marks on the drain that need to be sutured

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic instruments entering the field, beginning needle loading phase
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both instruments present and beginning to approach the suture

frame_analyses.description: Instruments manipulating suture to place through the first mark on the Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument positioning for precise suture placement

frame_analyses.description: Continuing suture placement through both marks on the drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears to be passing through both target marks accurately

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot with double wrap
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper formation of surgeon's knot initial throw with double wrap visible

frame_analyses.description: Completing and tightening the surgeon's knot first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tension control during knot tightening, appears secure

frame_analyses.description: Forming second throw with single wrap, hands switched from first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching technique demonstrated between throws

frame_analyses.description: Executing third and final throw with single wrap
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completing the knot sequence with appropriate hand switching

frame_analyses.description: Cutting suture ends with scissors inside the trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both suture ends being cut appropriately short but not too close to knot

frame_analyses.description: Final view showing completed sutured drain with knot in place
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Drain slit appears well closed with secure knot positioning

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw executed well
Single throw with proper hand switching from throw 1
Final single throw with proper hand switching from throw 2

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well-closed with good knot placement and no visible gaps

technique_summary: Excellent performance with proper surgeon's knot formation, correct hand switching between throws, and accurate suture placement. The knot sequence was executed efficiently with good tension control.
improvement_suggestions: Consider slightly faster needle loading to reduce overall time
Fine-tune suture placement to minimize deviation from marks
strengths: Secure knot formation with appropriate tension
